title: Fix aria2 large-file download tail collapse (Pod).
description: Big model weights on a Pod install download fast then drop to ~4 MB/s at 80-90% and crawl to the finish (seen on an LTX install onto an empty volume; also on other models). NOT the verify stage, NOT pip/decompress, NOT custom nodes (baked into image on the Pod). Root cause: aria2c flags in cubric-vision-pod/wrapper/wrapper.py:1526 = `-s 16 -k 16M`. `-s 16` caps TOTAL splits at 16; on a 40GB file, once early segments finish their connections go idle instead of subdividing the remaining tail, so the last 10-20% is dragged by 1-3 straggler connections = the 4 MB/s crawl (textbook aria2 large-file tail). Fix: raise `-s` (up to 64) and lower `-k` (4M) so aria2 keeps handing spare pieces to freed connections and can subdivide the tail. Pod-only (local engine uses single-stream node https, unaffected). Ships via wrapper R2 publish (publish-runtime.sh) + Pod restart — NO image rebuild. Original card title was 'move custom nodes to R2' — that premise was wrong (nodes baked on Pod); re-scoped to the real bug.
